ABS.d: Returns the absolute value of a number.

ACCRINT.d: Returns the accrued interest for a security that pays periodic interest.

ACCRINTM.d: Returns the accrued interest for a security that pays interest at maturity.

ACOS.d: Returns the arccosine of a number, in radians.

ACOSH.d: Returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine of a number.

ACOT.d: Returns the arccotangent of a number, in radians.

ACOTH.d: Returns the inverse hyperbolic cotangent of a number.

ADDRESS.d: Returns a cell address as text, given row and column numbers.

AGGREGATE.d: Returns an aggregate calculation (sum, average, etc.) while ignoring errors or hidden rows.

AND.d: Returns TRUE if all arguments are TRUE.

ARABIC.d: Converts a Roman numeral to an Arabic numeral.

AREAS.d: Returns the number of areas in a reference.

ARRAYTOTEXT.d: Returns an array of text values from any specified range.

ASC.d: Converts full-width characters to half-width characters.

ASIN.d: Returns the arcsine of a number, in radians.

ASINH.d: Returns the inverse hyperbolic sine of a number.

ATAN.d: Returns the arctangent of a number, in radians.

ATAN2.d: Returns the arctangent from x- and y-coordinates.

ATANH.d: Returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent of a number.

AVEDEV.d: Returns the average of the absolute deviations of data points from their mean.

AVERAGE.d: Returns the average (arithmetic mean) of its arguments.

AVERAGEA.d: Returns the average of its arguments, including numbers, text, and logical values.

AVERAGEIF.d: Returns the average of all cells in a range that meet a given criteria.

AVERAGEIFS.d: Returns the average of all cells that meet multiple criteria.

BASE.d: Converts a number into a text representation with the given base.

BESSELI.d: Returns the modified Bessel function In(x).

BESSELK.d: Returns the modified Bessel function Kn(x).

BETA.DIST.d: Returns the beta cumulative distribution function.

BETA.INV.d: Returns the inverse of the cumulative distribution function for a specified beta distribution.

BIN2DEC.d: Converts a binary number to decimal.

BIN2HEX.d: Converts a binary number to hexadecimal.

BIN2OCT.d: Converts a binary number to octal.

BINOM.DIST.d: Returns the individual term binomial distribution probability.

BINOM.DIST.RANGE.d: Returns the probability of a trial result using a binomial distribution.

BINOM.INV.d: Returns the smallest value for which the cumulative binomial distribution is less than or equal to a criterion value.

BITAND.d: Returns a bitwise AND of two numbers.

BITLSHIFT.d: Returns a number shifted left by shift_amount bits.

BITOR.d: Returns a bitwise OR of two numbers.

BITRSHIFT.d: Returns a number shifted right by shift_amount bits.

BITXOR.d: Returns a bitwise XOR of two numbers.

BYCOL.d: Applies a LAMBDA to each column and returns an array of the results.

BYROW.d: Applies a LAMBDA to each row and returns an array of the results.

CEILING.d: Rounds a number up to the nearest integer or nearest multiple of significance.

CEILING.MATH.d: Rounds a number up to the nearest integer or to the nearest multiple of significance.

CEILING.PRECISE.d: Rounds a number up to the nearest integer or to the nearest multiple of significance.

CELL.d: Returns information about the formatting, location, or contents of a cell.

CHAR.d: Returns the character specified by the code number.

CHISQ.DIST.RT.d: Returns the one-tailed probability of the chi-squared distribution.

CHISQ.INV.d: Returns the inverse of the left-tailed probability of the chi-squared distribution.

CHISQ.INV.RT.d: Returns the inverse of the one-tailed probability of the chi-squared distribution.

CHOOSE.d: Chooses a value from a list of values.

CHOOSECOLS.d: Returns the specified columns from an array.

CHOOSEROWS.d: Returns the specified rows from an array.

CLEAN.d: Removes all nonprintable characters from text.

CODE.d: Returns a numeric code for the first character in a text string.

COLUMN.d: Returns the column number of a reference.

COLUMNS.d: Returns the number of columns in a reference.

COMBIN.d: Returns the number of combinations for a given number of objects.

COMBINA.d: Returns the number of combinations with repetitions for a given number of items.

COMPLEX.d: Converts real and imaginary coefficients into a complex number.

CONCAT.d: Joins several text strings into one text string.

CONCATENATE.d: Joins several text strings into one text string.

CONFIDENCE.NORM.d: Returns the confidence interval for a population mean, using a normal distribution.

CONFIDENCE.T.d: Returns the confidence interval for a population mean, using a Student's t distribution.

CONVERT.d: Converts a number from one measurement system to another.

CORREL.d: Returns the correlation coefficient between two data sets.

COS.d: Returns the cosine of a number.

COSH.d: Returns the hyperbolic cosine of a number.

COT.d: Returns the cotangent of an angle.

COTH.d: Returns the hyperbolic cotangent of a number.

COUNT.d: Counts the cells in a range that contain numbers.

COUNTA.d: Counts the number of cells in a range that are not empty.

COUNTBLANK.d: Counts the number of empty cells in a range.

COUNTIF.d: Counts the number of cells in a range that meet a given condition.

COUNTIFS.d: Counts the number of cells in multiple ranges that meet multiple criteria.

COVARIANCE.P.d: Returns population covariance, the average of the products of deviations for each data point pair.

COVARIANCE.S.d: Returns the sample covariance, the average of the products of deviations for each data point pair.

CSC.d: Returns the cosecant of an angle.

CSCH.d: Returns the hyperbolic cosecant of an angle.

CUMIPMT.d: Returns the cumulative interest paid between two periods.

CUMPRINC.d: Returns the cumulative principal paid on a loan between two periods.

DATE.d: Returns the serial number of a particular date.

DATEDIF.d: Calculates the number of days, months, or years between two dates.

DATEVALUE.d: Converts a date in the form of text to a serial number.

DAVERAGE.d: Returns the average of selected database entries.

DAY.d: Converts a serial number to a day of the month.

DAYS.d: Returns the number of days between two dates.

DAYS360.d: Calculates the number of days between two dates based on a 360-day year.

DB.d: Returns the depreciation of an asset for a specified period using the fixed-declining balance method.

DCOUNT.d: Counts the cells that contain numbers in a database.

DCOUNTA.d: Counts non-empty cells in a database.

DDB.d: Returns the depreciation of an asset for a specified period using the double-declining balance method.

DEC2BIN.d: Converts a decimal number to binary.

DEC2HEX.d: Converts a decimal number to hexadecimal.

DEC2OCT.d: Converts a decimal number to octal.

DECIMAL.d: Converts a text representation of a number in a given base into a decimal number.

DELTA.d: Tests whether two values are equal.

DEVSQ.d: Returns the sum of squares of deviations.

DGET.d: Extracts from a database a single record that matches the specified criteria.

DMAX.d: Returns the maximum value from selected database entries.

DMIN.d: Returns the minimum value from selected database entries.

DOLLAR.d: Converts a number to text using the dollar currency format.

DOLLARDE.d: Converts a dollar price expressed as a fraction into a decimal number.

DOLLARFR.d: Converts a dollar price expressed as a decimal number into a fraction.

DPRODUCT.d: Multiplies the values in a field of records in a database that match the criteria.

DROP.d: Excludes a specified number of rows or columns from the start or end of an array.

DSTDEV.d: Estimates the standard deviation based on a sample of selected database entries.

DSTDEVP.d: Calculates the standard deviation based on the entire population of selected database entries.

DSUM.d: Adds the numbers in the field column of records in the database that match the criteria.

DVAR.d: Estimates variance based on a sample from selected database entries.

DVARP.d: Calculates variance based on the entire population of selected database entries.

EDATE.d: Returns the serial number of the date that is the indicated number of months before or after the start date.

EFFECT.d: Returns the effective annual interest rate.

EOMONTH.d: Returns the serial number of the last day of the month before or after a specified number of months.

ERROR.TYPE.d: Returns a number corresponding to one of the error values.

EVEN.d: Rounds a number up to the nearest even integer.

EXACT.d: Checks to see if two text values are identical.

EXP.d: Returns e raised to the power of a given number.

EXPAND.d: Expands or pads an array to specified row and column dimensions.

F.DIST.RT.d: Returns the right-tailed F probability distribution.

F.INV.d: Returns the inverse of the F probability distribution.

F.INV.RT.d: Returns the inverse of the right-tailed F probability distribution.

F.TEST.d: Returns the result of an F-test.

FACT.d: Returns the factorial of a number.

FACTDOUBLE.d: Returns the double factorial of a number.

FILTER.d: Filters a range of data based on criteria you define.

FIND.d: Finds one text string within another (case-sensitive).

FINDB.d: Finds one text string within another (case-sensitive, byte-based).

FISHERINV.d: Returns the inverse of the Fisher transformation.

FIXED.d: Formats a number as text with a fixed number of decimals.

FLOOR.d: Rounds a number down, toward zero.

FLOOR.MATH.d: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer or to the nearest multiple of significance.

FLOOR.PRECISE.d: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer or to the nearest multiple of significance.

FORMULATEXT.d: Returns the formula at the given reference as text.

FV.d: Returns the future value of an investment.

GAMMA.INV.d: Returns the inverse of the gamma cumulative distribution.

GAMMALN.d: Returns the natural logarithm of the gamma function.

GAMMALN.PRECISE.d: Returns the natural logarithm of the gamma function.

GAUSS.d: Returns 0.5 less than the standard normal cumulative distribution.

GESTEP.d: Tests whether a number is greater than a threshold value.

HEX2BIN.d: Converts a hexadecimal number to binary.

HEX2DEC.d: Converts a hexadecimal number to decimal.

HEX2OCT.d: Converts a hexadecimal number to octal.

HLOOKUP.d: Looks for a value in the top row of a table and returns the value in the same column from a row you specify.

HOUR.d: Converts a serial number to an hour.

HSTACK.d: Appends arrays horizontally and in sequence to return a larger array.

IF.d: Specifies a logical test to perform.

IFERROR.d: Returns a value you specify if a formula evaluates to an error.

IFNA.d: Returns the value you specify if the expression resolves to #N/A, otherwise returns the result of the expression.

IFS.d: Checks whether one or more conditions are met and returns a value that corresponds to the first TRUE condition.

IMABS.d: Returns the absolute value (modulus) of a complex number.

IMAGINARY.d: Returns the imaginary coefficient of a complex number.

IMARGUMENT.d: Returns the argument theta, an angle expressed in radians.

IMCONJUGATE.d: Returns the complex conjugate of a complex number.

IMCOS.d: Returns the cosine of a complex number.

IMCOSH.d: Returns the hyperbolic cosine of a complex number.

IMCOT.d: Returns the cotangent of a complex number.

IMCSC.d: Returns the cosecant of a complex number.

IMCSCH.d: Returns the hyperbolic cosecant of a complex number.

IMDIV.d: Returns the quotient of two complex numbers.

IMEXP.d: Returns the exponential of a complex number.

IMLN.d: Returns the natural logarithm of a complex number.

IMLOG10.d: Returns the base-10 logarithm of a complex number.

IMLOG2.d: Returns the base-2 logarithm of a complex number.

IMPOWER.d: Returns a complex number raised to an integer power.

IMPRODUCT.d: Returns the product of 2 to 255 complex numbers.

IMREAL.d: Returns the real coefficient of a complex number.

IMSEC.d: Returns the secant of a complex number.

IMSECH.d: Returns the hyperbolic secant of a complex number.

IMSIN.d: Returns the sine of a complex number.

IMSINH.d: Returns the hyperbolic sine of a complex number.

IMSQRT.d: Returns the square root of a complex number.

IMSUB.d: Returns the difference between two complex numbers.

IMSUM.d: Returns the sum of complex numbers.

IMTAN.d: Returns the tangent of a complex number.

INDEX.d: Uses an index to choose a value from a reference or array.

INDIRECT.d: Returns a reference indicated by a text value.

INFO.d: Returns information about the current operating environment.

INT.d: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer.

INTERCEPT.d: Returns the intercept of the linear regression line.

IPMT.d: Returns the interest payment for an investment for a given period.

IRR.d: Returns the internal rate of return for a series of cash flows.

ISBLANK.d: Returns TRUE if the value is blank.

ISERR.d: Returns TRUE if the value is any error value except #N/A.

ISERROR.d: Returns TRUE if the value is any error value.

ISEVEN.d: Returns TRUE if the number is even.

ISFORMULA.d: Returns TRUE if there is a formula in the referenced cell.

ISLOGICAL.d: Returns TRUE if the value is a logical value.

ISNA.d: Returns TRUE if the value is the #N/A error value.

ISNONTEXT.d: Returns TRUE if the value is not text.

ISNUMBER.d: Returns TRUE if the value is a number.

ISO.CEILING.d: Returns a number that is rounded up to the nearest integer or to the nearest multiple of significance.

ISODD.d: Returns TRUE if the number is odd.

ISOMITTED.d: Checks whether the value in a LAMBDA is missing and returns TRUE or FALSE.

ISOWEEKNUM.d: Returns the number of the ISO week number of the year for a given date.

ISPMT.d: Calculates the interest paid during a specific period of an investment.

ISREF.d: Returns TRUE if the value is a reference.

ISTEXT.d: Returns TRUE if the value is text.

KURT.d: Returns the kurtosis of a data set.

LAMBDA.d: Creates a custom reusable function with named parameters.

LARGE.d: Returns the k-th largest value in a data set.

LEFT.d: Returns the leftmost characters from a text value.

LEFTB.d: Returns the leftmost characters from a text value (byte-based).

LEN.d: Returns the number of characters in a text string.

LENB.d: Returns the number of bytes used to represent the characters in a text string.

LET.d: Assigns names to calculation results and returns the final formula.

LN.d: Returns the natural logarithm of a number.

LOG.d: Returns the logarithm of a number to a specified base.

LOG10.d: Returns the base-10 logarithm of a number.

LOGNORM.DIST.d: Returns the lognormal distribution of x.

LOGNORM.INV.d: Returns the inverse of the lognormal cumulative distribution function.

LOOKUP.d: Looks up values in a vector or array.

MAP.d: Returns an array formed by mapping each value in the array to a new value by applying a LAMBDA.

MATCH.d: Looks up values in a reference or array.

MAX.d: Returns the largest value in a set of values.

MAXA.d: Returns the maximum value in a list of arguments, including numbers, text, and logical values.

MAXIFS.d: Returns the maximum value among cells specified by a given set of conditions or criteria.

MEDIAN.d: Returns the median of the given numbers.

MID.d: Returns a specific number of characters from a text string, starting at the position you specify.

MIDB.d: Returns a specific number of characters from a text string, starting at the position you specify (byte-based).

MIN.d: Returns the smallest value in a set of values.

MINA.d: Returns the smallest value in a list of arguments, including numbers, text, and logical values.

MINIFS.d: Returns the minimum value among cells specified by a given set of conditions or criteria.

MINUTE.d: Converts a serial number to a minute.

MIRR.d: Returns the internal rate of return where positive and negative cash flows are financed at different rates.

MONTH.d: Converts a serial number to a month.

MROUND.d: Returns a number rounded to the desired multiple.

N.d: Returns a value converted to a number.

NETWORKDAYS.d: Returns the number of whole workdays between two dates.

NETWORKDAYS.INTL.d: Returns the number of whole workdays between two dates using parameters to indicate which days are weekend days.

NOMINAL.d: Returns the annual nominal interest rate.

NORM.INV.d: Returns the inverse of the normal cumulative distribution.

NORM.S.DIST.d: Returns the standard normal cumulative distribution.

NORM.S.INV.d: Returns the inverse of the standard normal cumulative distribution.

NOT.d: Reverses the logic of its argument.

NOW.d: Returns the serial number of the current date and time.

NPER.d: Returns the number of periods for an investment.

NPV.d: Returns the net present value of an investment based on a series of periodic cash flows and a discount rate.

NUMBERVALUE.d: Converts text to a number in a locale-independent manner.

OCT2BIN.d: Converts an octal number to binary.

OCT2DEC.d: Converts an octal number to decimal.

OCT2HEX.d: Converts an octal number to hexadecimal.

ODD.d: Rounds a number up to the nearest odd integer.

OFFSET.d: Returns a reference offset from a given reference.

OR.d: Returns TRUE if any argument is TRUE.

PDURATION.d: Returns the number of periods required by an investment to reach a specified value.

PEARSON.d: Returns the Pearson product moment correlation coefficient.

PHI.d: Returns the value of the density function for a standard normal distribution.

PMT.d: Returns the periodic payment for an annuity.

POWER.d: Returns the result of a number raised to a power.

PPMT.d: Returns the payment on the principal for an investment for a given period.

PROPER.d: Capitalizes the first letter in each word of a text value.

PV.d: Returns the present value of an investment.

QUOTIENT.d: Returns the integer portion of a division.

RAND.d: Returns a random number between 0 and 1.

RANDARRAY.d: Returns an array of random numbers.

RANDBETWEEN.d: Returns a random integer between two numbers.

RANK.AVG.d: Returns the rank of a number in a list of numbers.

RANK.EQ.d: Returns the rank of a number in a list of numbers.

RATE.d: Returns the interest rate per period of an annuity.

REDUCE.d: Reduces an array to an accumulated value by applying a LAMBDA to each value.

REGEXEXTRACT.d: Extracts matching substrings according to a regular expression.

REGEXREPLACE.d: Replaces part of a text string with a different text string using regular expressions.

REGEXTEST.d: Tests whether any part of provided text matches a regular expression.

REPT.d: Repeats text a given number of times.

RIGHT.d: Returns the rightmost characters from a text value.

RIGHTB.d: Returns the rightmost characters from a text value (byte-based).

ROMAN.d: Converts an Arabic numeral to Roman, as text.

ROUND.d: Rounds a number to a specified number of digits.

ROUNDDOWN.d: Rounds a number down, toward zero.

ROUNDUP.d: Rounds a number up, away from zero.

ROW.d: Returns the row number of a reference.

ROWS.d: Returns the number of rows in a reference.

RRI.d: Returns an equivalent interest rate for the growth of an investment.

RSQ.d: Returns the square of the Pearson product moment correlation coefficient.

SCAN.d: Scans an array by applying a LAMBDA to each value and returns an array that has each intermediate value.

SEARCH.d: Finds one text value within another (not case-sensitive).

SEARCHB.d: Finds one text value within another (not case-sensitive, byte-based).

SEC.d: Returns the secant of an angle.

SECH.d: Returns the hyperbolic secant of an angle.

SECOND.d: Converts a serial number to a second.

SEQUENCE.d: Generates a list of sequential numbers in an array.

SHEET.d: Returns the sheet number of the referenced sheet.

SHEETS.d: Returns the number of sheets in a reference.

SIGN.d: Returns the sign of a number.

SIN.d: Returns the sine of the given angle.

SINH.d: Returns the hyperbolic sine of a number.

SKEW.d: Returns the skewness of a distribution.

SKEW.P.d: Returns the skewness of a distribution based on a population.

SLOPE.d: Returns the slope of the linear regression line.

SMALL.d: Returns the k-th smallest value in a data set.

SORT.d: Sorts the contents of a range or array.

SORTBY.d: Sorts the contents of a range or array based on the values in a corresponding range or array.

SQRTPI.d: Returns the square root of (number * pi).

STANDARDIZE.d: Returns a normalized value from a distribution characterized by mean and standard_dev.

STDEV.P.d: Calculates standard deviation based on the entire population.

STDEV.S.d: Estimates standard deviation based on a sample.

STDEVA.d: Estimates standard deviation based on a sample, including numbers, text, and logical values.

STDEVPA.d: Calculates standard deviation based on the entire population, including numbers, text, and logical values.

STEYX.d: Returns the standard error of the predicted y-value for each x in the regression.

SUBSTITUTE.d: Substitutes new text for old text in a text string.

SUBTOTAL.d: Returns a subtotal in a list or database.

SUM.d: Adds all the numbers in a range of cells.

SUMIF.d: Adds the cells specified by a given condition or criteria.

SUMIFS.d: Adds the cells in a range that meet multiple criteria.

SUMX2MY2.d: Returns the sum of the difference of squares of corresponding values in two arrays.

SUMX2PY2.d: Returns the sum of the sum of squares of corresponding values in two arrays.

SUMXMY2.d: Returns the sum of squares of differences of corresponding values in two arrays.

SUMSQ.d: Returns the sum of the squares of the arguments.

SWITCH.d: Evaluates an expression against a list of values and returns the result corresponding to the first matching value.

T.INV.d: Returns the left-tailed inverse of the Student's t-distribution.

T.INV.2T.d: Returns the two-tailed inverse of the Student's t-distribution.

T.TEST.d: Returns the probability associated with a Student's t-test.

TAKE.d: Returns a specified number of contiguous rows or columns from the start or end of an array.

TAN.d: Returns the tangent of a number.

TANH.d: Returns the hyperbolic tangent of a number.

TBILLEQ.d: Returns the bond-equivalent yield for a Treasury bill.

TBILLPRICE.d: Returns the price per $100 face value for a Treasury bill.

TBILLYIELD.d: Returns the yield for a Treasury bill.

TEXT.d: Formats a number and converts it to text.

TEXTAFTER.d: Returns text that occurs after a given character or string.

TEXTBEFORE.d: Returns text that occurs before a given character or string.

TEXTJOIN.d: Combines the text from multiple ranges and/or strings, with a delimiter you specify.

TEXTSPLIT.d: Splits text strings by using column and row delimiters.

TIME.d: Returns the serial number of a particular time.

TIMEVALUE.d: Converts a time in the form of text to a serial number.

TODAY.d: Returns the serial number of today's date.

TOCOL.d: Returns the array in a single column.

TOROW.d: Returns the array in a single row.

TRANSPOSE.d: Returns the transpose of an array.

TRIMRANGE.d: Trims empty rows and columns from the edges of an array.

TRUNC.d: Truncates a number to an integer.

TYPE.d: Returns a number indicating the data type of a value.

UNICHAR.d: Returns the Unicode character that is referenced by the given numeric value.

UNICODE.d: Returns the number (code point) of the first character of the text.

UNIQUE.d: Returns a list of unique values in a list or range.

VALUE.d: Converts a text argument to a number.

VALUETOTEXT.d: Returns text from any specified value.

VAR.P.d: Calculates variance based on the entire population.

VAR.S.d: Estimates variance based on a sample.

VARA.d: Estimates variance based on a sample, including numbers, text, and logical values.

VARPA.d: Calculates variance based on the entire population, including numbers, text, and logical values.

VLOOKUP.d: Looks in the first column of an array and moves across the row to return the value of a cell.

VSTACK.d: Appends arrays vertically and in sequence to return a larger array.

WEEKDAY.d: Converts a serial number to a day of the week.

WEEKNUM.d: Converts a serial number to a number representing where the week falls numerically with a year.

WORKDAY.d: Returns the serial number of the date before or after a specified number of workdays.

WORKDAY.INTL.d: Returns the serial number of the date before or after a specified number of workdays using parameters to indicate which days are weekend days.

WRAPCOLS.d: Wraps the provided row or column of values by columns after a specified number of elements.

WRAPROWS.d: Wraps the provided row or column of values by rows after a specified number of elements.

XIRR.d: Returns the internal rate of return for a schedule of cash flows that is not necessarily periodic.

XLOOKUP.d: Searches a range or an array, and returns an item corresponding to the first match it finds.

XMATCH.d: Returns the relative position of an item in an array or range of cells.

XNPV.d: Returns the net present value for a schedule of cash flows that is not necessarily periodic.

XOR.d: Returns a logical Exclusive Or of all arguments.

YEAR.d: Converts a serial number to a year.

YEARFRAC.d: Returns the year fraction representing the number of whole days between start_date and end_date.

Z.TEST.d: Returns the one-tailed probability-value of a z-test.
